preamble: The voiced alveolar affricate [dz] can undergo a phonetic transformation to the alveolar lateral approximant [l]. This shift represents a process of lenition, where a more complex and obstructive sound simplifies to a less obstructed one. This particular sound change has been observed in certain dialects of Taiwanese Southern Min, also known as Taiwanese Hokkien.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves a change in the manner of articulation from an affricate to an approximant, and a change in the airflow from central to lateral

examples.note: Observed in the 'Ru/Ru 入入' initial group, particularly in the Kaohsiung accent and Sikci dialect. Specific word examples were not provided in the research snippets.
